body.visualDesc: Medium-wide shot under the Big Oak at dusk: THREE chicks arranged left, center and right around a golden straw nest, all frozen mid-motion, holding their breath. LEFT chick: Mo - blue-grey feathers, chunky red knitted scarf, tall red comb, sitting motionless with folded wings. CENTER chick: Bo - tan sandy-brown feathers, round thin wire-framed eyeglasses on her beak, green neck scarf, both wings pressed against her own cheeks in suspense. RIGHT chick: Pip - cream-white feathers, straw farmer hat, red neck bandana, one wing hovering frozen mid-air above the egg. In the nest: the pale-cream egg, shell CLOSED, two thin hairline cracks with a faint warm glow seeping through. Count check: three chicks, one egg, nothing else alive in frame. Golden-amber dusk light, first fireflies. Absolute stillness.
